sections.instruction: Questions 21-25: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 26-30: Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter A-G next to questions 26-30.

questions.content: Russ says that his difficulty in planning the presentation is due to
options: A his lack of knowledge about the topic.
B his uncertainty about what he should try to achieve.
C the short time that he has for preparation.

questions.content: Russ and his tutor agree that his approach in the presentation will be
options: A to concentrate on how nanotechnology is used in one field.
B to follow the chronological development of nanotechnology.
C to show the range of applications of nanotechnology.

questions.content: In connection with slides, the tutor advises Russ to
options: A talk about things that he can find slides to illustrate.
B look for slides to illustrate the points he makes.

questions.content: They both agree that the best way for Russ to start his presentation is
options: A to encourage the audience to talk.
B to explain what Russ intends to do.

questions.content: What does the tutor advise Russ to do next while preparing his presentation?
options: A summarise the main point he wants to make
B read the notes he has already made
C list the topics he wants to cover

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.content:
• The ability to recall details e.g. the time and {{BLANK}} of past events
• Different to semantic memory- the ability to remember general information about the {{BLANK}} which does not involve recalling {{BLANK}} information
Forming episodic memories involves three steps:
Encoding
• Involves receiving and processing information
• The more {{BLANK}} given to an event the more successfully it can be encoded
• To remember a {{BLANK}} it is useful to have a strategy for encoding such information
Consolidation
• How memories are strengthened and stored
• Most effective when memories can be added to a {{BLANK}}
• The {{BLANK}} of retrieval affects the strength of memories
Retrieval
• Memory retrieval often depends on using a prompt e.g. the {{BLANK}} of an object near to the place where you left your car
Episodic memory impairments
• These affect people with a wide range of medical conditions
• Games which stimulate the {{BLANK}} have been found to help people with schizophrenia
• Children with autism may have difficulty forming episodic memories – possibly because their concept of the {{BLANK}} may be absent
• Memory training may help autistic children to develop social skills
